mechanism_narrative: A sustained near-zero federal funds rate compresses the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, raising present values and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 5.0% indicates persistent labor market slack that weakens wage growth and consumer spending, reducing corporate revenue expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated retail sales combined with low federal funds rates reflect strong consumer demand financed by accommodative credit conditions, jointly lifting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and FEDFUNDS fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.05) or joint high retail sales and low rate periods show negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Back-to-back monthly declines in industrial production during late-cycle expansions signal deteriorating manufacturing momentum and pricing power, triggering de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of late-cycle observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly tracks the economy's productive output, and positive momentum in GDPC1 signals expanding aggregate demand that lifts broad corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth quarters are followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation rate above 2.5% accelerates the Federal Reserve's normalization timeline, raises discount rate expectations and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 2.5% year-over-year are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding labor income and household consumption capacity, translating into higher expected corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Positive monthly changes in PAYEMS are followed by negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1.2 million units indicate strong residential investment and construction sector momentum, spilling over into durable goods demand and employment, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST readings above 1200 are followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.
